commit.message:
Feat/custom roles implementation (#60)

* feat(db): add custom roles, scope columns, and scoped RLS policies

Evolve app_role enum with zone_leader, zone_contributor, and
community_responsible roles. Add person_id, zone_id, community_id
scope columns to profiles with consistency constraints.

Create authorization helper functions (can_access_community,
can_manage_community, can_manage_brothers, etc.) and update
all RLS policies to enforce zone/community scoping.

Update set_user_role, get_admin_users_list, and merge_communities
RPCs to handle the new roles and scope fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add permissions utility and expose user scope in AuthContext

Create lib/permissions.ts with role definitions, labels, badge styles,
and permission check functions for all community actions.

Update AuthContext to fetch user scope (role, zone_id, community_id)
via get_user_scope() RPC and expose it to all components.

Update useAdminUsers hook to support the new role types and scope
parameters (person_id, zone_id, community_id).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ui): enforce role-based visibility in sidebar, community detail, and admin

Filter sidebar navigation groups by role using getSidebarVisibility().
community_responsible sees only Inicio and Comunidades.

Guard community detail actions (edit, delete, merge, print, audit,
teams, brothers) based on permissions. Hide buttons and modals for
unauthorized roles. BrothersList hides add/delete buttons when
callbacks are undefined.

Extend admin page with all 6 roles, scope assignment UI (zone/community
selectors), and scope display column in users table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add route protection layout for unauthorized URL access

Create (protected)/layout.tsx that checks canAccessRoute() against
the current pathname and redirects to home if the user's role doesn't
have access. Add canAccessRoute() to permissions.ts mapping pathnames
to sidebar visibility rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-redirect community_responsible to their community on home

When a community_responsible user visits the home page, redirect them
to their assigned community detail page. Also filter quick access and
secondary cards on home page by role visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(db): allow community_responsible to edit people in their community

Update the people UPDATE RLS policy so community_responsible can edit
people records that are brothers in their assigned community. This
enables updating phone, email, and other person details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): wrap storage setup in conditional block for db reset compatibility

The storage schema doesn't exist during `supabase db reset` because
it's created after migrations run. Wrap the storage bucket and policy
creation in a DO block that checks for the storage schema first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(db): resolve migration errors for db reset compatibility

- Move storage bucket setup from SQL to config.toml (storage schema
  doesn't exist during db reset)
- Split ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE into separate migration
  (20260305000000) since PostgreSQL requires new enum values to be
  committed before they can be referenced
- DROP old function signatures before recreating set_user_role and
  get_admin_users_list (return type / parameter changes require drop)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: make viewer role scoped — requires zone or community assignment

Viewer is no longer a global read-all role. A new user (viewer with
no scope) sees nothing until an admin assigns them a zone or community.

DB changes:
- Update can_access_community() to treat viewer like scoped roles
- Update all SELECT RLS policies to remove viewer from global list
- Update set_user_role() to preserve optional scope for viewer

Frontend changes:
- Viewer without scope sees only home page (empty state)
- Viewer with community_id redirects to their community on home
- Viewer with zone_id sees comunidades + parroquias in sidebar
- Admin page shows optional zone/community selectors for viewer
- Print/step log permissions gated on viewer having scope

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: implement community access management

- Added CommunityAccessSection component for managing user access to communities.
- Introduced useCommunityAccess hook for fetching and modifying community access users.
- Created new Supabase functions for granting and revoking community access.
- Updated permissions to include community grants in user scope.
- Enhanced user interface with dialogs for adding and revoking access.
- Added version check for app updates and download link in NavUser component.
- Removed DownloadApp component from AppSidebar.
- Updated TeamSection to conditionally render delete buttons based on onDelete prop.
- Added new SQL migrations for user community access management and scope updates.

* feat: enhance useAppVersion hook with electron detection and version comparison

---------

commit.message:
Fix OAuth gray screen and iPad touch input (v2)

Root causes:
- CanvasLayer bypasses viewport stretch transform, breaking control
  positions on iPad (960x540 viewport maps to different screen size)
- No visual feedback during OAuth wait in start_screen
- grab_focus on web triggers unwanted virtual keyboard on iPad
- Potential race: auth_changed could emit before scene connects

Fixes:
- Remove CanvasLayer, keep controls as Node2D children (correct coords)
- Add mouse_filter=IGNORE on all labels/decorative Controls
- Show "Signing in..." screen during OAuth wait instead of gray
- Only grab_focus on non-web (desktop) builds
- Use call_deferred for auth_changed emission in OAuth callback
- Guard _await_first timer disconnect with is_instance_valid

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent worker crash: skip OCR for PDFs > 5MB, catch pymupdf errors

Large PDFs sent to dotsocr were causing worker OOM crashes. Now skips
OCR for files > 5MB (uses whatever pymupdf extracted). Also wraps
pymupdf in try/except to handle corrupt PDFs gracefully.

commit.message:
feat: v3 — migrate to registered claude_code custom tool

Replace exec-based delegation with a proper plugin tool registered via
api.registerTool(). The LLM now calls claude_code({task, workdir}) directly
with structured parameters instead of constructing shell commands.

New files: lib/tool.ts (claude_code factory), lib/process-tool.ts
(claude_code_status for background polling), lib/semaphore.ts (concurrency).
Simplified INJECT.md and SKILL.md to reference the tool API.

Fix multi-node drag: preserve selection when clicking selected node

Previously, clicking a node without Shift always reset the selection
to just that node, breaking multi-drag. Now clicking an already-selected
node within a group keeps the group selection intact for dragging.
Also improved Shift+click to toggle selection instead of only adding.

commit.message:
Expand compat tests wave 3: EC2, Glue, SageMaker + 7 more services (+3204 lines)

EC2 batch 2: network ACLs, NAT gateways, VPN/customer gateways, VPC peering,
snapshots, volumes, spot/reserved instances, network interfaces, instance ops.
Glue batch 2: job/crawler/database/table/connection/schema/registry/trigger/
workflow/session CRUD, partitions, tags, blueprints.
SageMaker: model/endpoint/notebook/training/transform/HPT/processing/model
package/model card/experiment/trial CRUD, tags.
SecurityHub: hub, standards, findings, insights, action targets, members.
Redshift: snapshots, scheduled actions, usage limits, data shares.
SESv2: suppression, import jobs, verification templates, send email.
DS: directory/snapshot CRUD, tags, settings.
Macie2: session, classification jobs, findings filters, allow lists.
Rekognition: collections, projects, stream processors, tags.
Transfer: servers, users, connectors, workflows, agreements, tags.

feat(AADS-186C): Langfuse Observability + FastAPI-MCP + Telegram 알림 봇

Part 1 — Langfuse 셀프호스팅:
- docker-compose.langfuse.yml: Langfuse v3 + 별도 PostgreSQL (langfuse_db), 포트 3001
- scripts/setup_langfuse.sh: 컨테이너 기동 + 헬스체크 대기 + 안내 출력
- app/core/langfuse_config.py: SDK 초기화, LiteLLM 콜백, create_trace(), graceful 비활성화
- app/services/chat_service.py: send_message_stream 에 intent/llm_generation span 추가

Part 2 — FastAPI-MCP:
- app/core/mcp_server.py: FastApiMCP 마운트, MCP_ENABLED=false 시 skip
- app/main.py: setup_mcp(app) 등록

Part 3 — Telegram 알림 봇:
- migrations/023_alert_history.sql: alert_history 테이블 + 인덱스
- app/services/alert_manager.py: RULES 8개, evaluate_rules, send_alert, 1시간 중복방지
- app/services/telegram_bot.py: TelegramBot (send_alert, send_daily_summary, handle_command)
- app/main.py: APScheduler (2분 주기 알림평가, 09:00 KST 일일요약), Langfuse/Telegram lifespan

공통:
- pyproject.toml: apscheduler, langfuse, fastapi-mcp, python-telegram-bot 추가
- .env.example / docker-compose.yml: 신규 환경변수 추가

commit.message:
style: one statement per line, fix validation j=0 skips

Split all semicolon-joined statements onto separate lines (tidyverse
style) in fit-gmm.R, ivreg2.R, and validate-helpfile.qmd. Added
one-statement-per-line rule to CLAUDE.md Code Style section.

Also treat Stata's j=0/jdf=0 placeholder for exactly-identified models
as matching R's NULL in compare_fixture(), eliminating ~30 false skips
in validation output.

commit.message:
Robust usage API error handling and startup data loading

- Handle 429 rate limit from usage API: re-emit last known good data
  instead of failing silently and leaving the tooltip stale
- Load cached usage from disk on tracker startup so tooltip has data
  immediately, even if the first API call fails
- Add _loadCachedUsage() helper for disk-based fallback
- Increase default poll interval from 1min to 2min to reduce 429s
- If no keychain credentials on startup, still emit cached data
- Pass details field through normalizeUsageData for downstream use

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Add dynamic blending, time-decay training, style matchups, and walk-forward validation

- Style matchup features: striker_edge and grappler_edge interactions
- Weight class move detection for fighters changing divisions
- Cage rust features: layoff flag (>365d) and log-scaled duration

- Results: 135 bets, 61.5% win rate, +18.0% ROI, +$1,064 profit, +5.37% CLV

feat(http-proxy): Gap 2/4 — wire ACIS envelopes into 117 secondary decision sites (HTTP+WS+gRPC)

All security-decision log_entry() calls across HTTP, WebSocket, and gRPC
handlers now use log_entry_with_acis() with proper DecisionOrigin:

HTTP handlers.rs (42 sites):
  DLP→Dlp, injection→InjectionScanner, memory poisoning→MemoryPoisoning,
  circuit breaker→RateLimiter, rug-pull→CapabilityEnforcement,
  ABAC/privilege/gateway→PolicyEngine, session→SessionGuard

WebSocket mod.rs (40 sites):
  DLP→Dlp, injection→InjectionScanner, memory poisoning→MemoryPoisoning,
  circuit breaker→RateLimiter, rug-pull→CapabilityEnforcement,
  ABAC/privilege/schema→PolicyEngine

gRPC service.rs (35 sites):
  DLP→Dlp, injection→InjectionScanner, memory poisoning→MemoryPoisoning,
  circuit breaker→RateLimiter, rug-pull→CapabilityEnforcement,
  ABAC/privilege/schema→PolicyEngine

Combined with the 77 stdio relay sites (a6d95788), this completes
Gap 2+4: 194 total secondary ACIS decision sites across all 4 transports.
Every security decision now has a structured ACIS envelope with session
binding for cross-session audit coherence.

commit.message:
fix(mcp): R246-RELAY-5 — scan result.contents[] in DLP and injection scanners

MCP resources/read responses use result.contents[] (not result.content[]
which is for tool call responses). Both scan_response_for_secrets (DLP)
and extract_response_text (injection) were only scanning result.content[],
meaning resource read responses bypassed both DLP and injection scanning
entirely in the response path.

Added scanning for all contents[] fields: text, blob (base64-decoded),
uri, name, description, mimeType, and annotations.

Also fixed 20 call sites for the session_id parameter added to
presented_approval_matches_action, consume_presented_approval, and
create_pending_approval by the R246-RELAY-1 session binding fix.

4 new tests, 0 failures, 0 clippy warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix deploy script: use nix copy for remote cache

The nix daemon can't access user SSH keys, so ssh-ng substituters
fail. Instead, use `nix copy --from ssh://` to pull the closure
directly, then nixos-rebuild finds everything in the local store.
